summary: Mass include-path cleanup across src/sysdolphin/baselib to work around an MWCC `-cwd source` header-resolution bug. The PR replaced many angle-bracket baselib includes such as `#include <baselib/jobj.h>` with quoted includes that are either local to the same baselib directory, such as `#include "jobj.h"`, or explicitly rooted as `#include "baselib/jobj.h"` inside headers. This mattered because MWCC resolves quoted includes from the current C file location rather than from the including header's directory when `-cwd source` is used, so insufficiently specific header paths could make unrelated translation units search the wrong directory, e.g. `fighter.c` including `gobj.h` then trying to resolve `melee/ft/jobj.h` instead of the baselib header.
what_changed: Changed many sysdolphin baselib implementation-file includes from global-style `#include <baselib/...>` to local quoted includes like `#include "class.h"`, `#include "gobj.h"`, `#include "debug.h"`, and `#include "objalloc.h"`.

Changed many sysdolphin baselib header-file dependencies from angle-bracket baselib paths to quoted explicit baselib paths such as `#include "baselib/forward.h"`, `#include "baselib/jobj.h"`, `#include "baselib/objalloc.h"`, and `#include "baselib/archive.h"`.

Preserved external SDK/library include style for platform, Dolphin, MetroTRK, MSL, and other non-baselib headers such as `<platform.h>`, `<dolphin/os.h>`, `<dolphin/gx/GXAttr.h>`, and `<MSL/trigf.h>`.
Touched 45 files with nearly one-for-one include edits, totaling 169 added and 171 deleted lines according to the PR context.
Removed `#include <baselib/psdisptev.h>` from `src/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisptev.c` in the excerpt, leaving `<platform.h>` and extern/function declarations visible there.
smart_moves: Applied a consistent rule that C files in `src/sysdolphin/baselib` should include sibling baselib headers with short quoted names, avoiding unnecessary global include lookup.

Applied a different rule for headers: when a baselib header includes another baselib header, keep the path explicit as `"baselib/..."` so translation units outside baselib do not accidentally resolve relative to their own source directory.
Kept system and SDK headers in angle brackets, making the intended distinction between project-local baselib headers and external include roots clear.
The PR body documented the MWCC behavior with a concrete failure mode involving `gobj.h`, `jobj.h`, and `fighter.c`, making the otherwise mechanical include churn easy to audit and reuse.
decomp_lessons: With MWCC `-cwd source`, do not assume a quoted include inside a header resolves relative to that header. The PR states MWCC uses the location of the current C file, not the header, so project headers need paths that remain valid from downstream translation-unit directories.
For sysdolphin baselib C files, prefer same-directory quoted includes for sibling headers, e.g. `#include "jobj.h"`, because the current C file is already in `src/sysdolphin/baselib`.

For sysdolphin baselib headers that may be included from outside baselib, prefer quoted but path-qualified includes, e.g. `#include "baselib/jobj.h"`, to avoid accidentally searching directories like `melee/ft/`.
Include-path-only PRs can still be important decomp infrastructure fixes: they stabilize builds and prevent header-resolution bugs without changing function bodies or matching logic.
When doing broad include cleanup, preserve external header conventions so the diff communicates which headers are local project headers versus SDK/toolchain headers.
naming_conventions: Baselib headers are referenced in C files by sibling names such as `"class.h"`, `"debug.h"`, `"gobj.h"`, `"jobj.h"`, `"pobj.h"`, `"robj.h"`, and `"tev.h"`.

Baselib headers are referenced from other headers with a quoted `baselib/` prefix, e.g. `"baselib/forward.h"`, `"baselib/jobj.h"`, `"baselib/gobj.h"`, `"baselib/archive.h"`, and `"baselib/objalloc.h"`.
External or configured-root headers remained in angle brackets, including `<platform.h>`, `<dolphin/...>`, `<MetroTRK/intrinsics.h>`, `<MSL/trigf.h>`, `<__mem.h>`, and `<Gecko_setjmp.h>`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching changes are evidenced; the diff excerpt is an include-path/build-compatibility cleanup.
The matching-relevant tactic is indirect: avoid MWCC `-cwd source` include-resolution pitfalls so translation units compile with the intended headers before attempting function matching.
The PR did not provide evidence of new symbols, struct layout changes, or asm tricks.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.

The only explicit rationale is from the PR body: MWCC has a bug under `-cwd source` where it uses the current C file location rather than the header location for include resolution, requiring more specific header paths.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching sysdolphin baselib include style to see whether the C-file short quoted include and header `"baselib/..."` convention remained stable.
Search for remaining `#include <baselib/` occurrences after PR 1120 to identify files that may still be vulnerable to the MWCC `-cwd source` behavior.
Look for other documented MWCC include-resolution or `-cwd source` workarounds in past PRs.
Check whether non-sysdolphin headers that are included from fighter or other game directories follow the same explicit quoted path convention.
